channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: UK warns of Chinese hackers using proxy networks to evade detection
item.description: The United Kingdom's National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC-UK) and international partners warned that China-nexus hackers are increasingly using large-scale proxy networks of hijacked consumer devices ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New GopherWhisper APT group abuses Outlook, Slack, Discord for comms
item.description: A previously undocumented state-backed threat actor named GopherWhisper is using a Go-based custom toolkit and legitimate services like Microsoft 365 Outlook, Slack, and Discord in attacks against gov...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: [Webinar] Mythos Reality Check: Beating Automated Exploitation at AI Speed
item.description: Imagine a world where hackers don't sleep, don't take breaks, and find weak spots in your systems instantly. Well, that world is already here. Thanks to AI, attackers are now launching automated, larg...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Project Glasswing Proved AI Can Find the Bugs. Who's Going to Fix Them?
item.description: Last week, Anthropic announced Project Glasswing, an AI model so effective at discovering software vulnerabilities that they took the extraordinary step of postponing its public release. Instead, the ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA orders feds to patch BlueHammer flaw exploited as zero-day
item.description: CISA has ordered U.S. federal agencies to patch a Microsoft Defender privilege escalation flaw (dubbed BlueHammer) that has been exploited in zero-day attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 'Zealot' Shows What AI's Capable of in Staged Cloud Attack
item.description: The proof of concept revealed AI-based attacks unfold too fast for human defenders to respond, and that AI evinced more autonomous behavior than expected....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: China-Linked GopherWhisper Infects 12 Mongolian Government Systems with Go Backdoors
item.description: Mongolian governmental institutions have emerged as the target of a previously undocumented China-aligned advanced persistent threat (APT) group tracked as GopherWhisper. "The group wields a wide arra...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Vercel Finds More Compromised Accounts in Context.ai-Linked Breach
item.description: Vercel on Wednesday revealed that it has identified an additional set of customer accounts that were compromised as part of a security incident that enabled unauthorized access to its internal systems...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Apple Fixes iOS Flaw That Let FBI Recover Deleted Signal Messages
item.description: Apple has rolled out a software fix for iOS and iPadOS to address a Notification Services flaw that stored notifications marked for deletion on the device. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-28950...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Apple fixes bug that let the FBI recover deleted Signal messages
item.description: Apple has released out-of-band security updates for iPhone and iPad devices to fix a Notification Services flaw that could allow notifications marked for deletion to remain stored on the device. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 'The Gentlemen' Rapidly Rises to Ransomware Prominence
item.description: Not nearly as polite as the name suggests, the ransomware gang has impressed researchers with its speed in scaling up operations — and its sophistication....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New Mirai campaign exploits RCE flaw in EoL D-Link routers
item.description: A new Mirai-based malware campaign is actively exploiting CVE-2025-29635, a high-severity command-injection vulnerability affecting D-Link DIR-823X routers, to enlist devices into the botnet. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Kyber ransomware gang toys with post-quantum encryption on Windows
item.description: A new Kyber ransomware operation is targeting Windows systems and VMware ESXi endpoints in recent attacks, with one variant implementing Kyber1024 post-quantum encryption. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Malicious KICS Docker Images and VS Code Extensions Hit Checkmarx Supply Chain
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of malicious images pushed to the official "checkmarx/kics" Docker Hub repository. In an alert published today, software supply chain security company Socket reve...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Self-Propagating Supply Chain Worm Hijacks npm Packages to Steal Developer Tokens
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a fresh set of packages that have been compromised by bad actors to deliver a self-propagating worm that spreads through stolen developer npm tokens. The supply ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Harvester Deploys Linux GoGra Backdoor in South Asia Using Microsoft Graph API
item.description: The threat actor known as Harvester has been attributed to a new Linux version of its GoGra backdoor deployed as part of attacks likely targeting entities in South Asia. "The malware uses the legitima...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Spain dismantles major $4.7M manga piracy platform, arrests four
item.description: The Spanish police have dismantled the largest Spanish-language manga piracy platform, operating since 2014, with millions of monthly users from around the globe. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: DPRK Fake Job Scams Self-Propagate in 'Contagious Interview'
item.description: A compromised developer's repository serves as a worm-like infection vector to spread remote access Trojans (RATs) and other malware....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Inside Caller-as-a-Service Fraud: The Scam Economy Has a Hiring Process
item.description: Fraud operations now operate like call centers, complete with hiring, training, and performance tracking. Flare reveals how cybercriminals manage "Caller-as-a-Service" operations like a professional s...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New npm supply-chain attack self-spreads to steal auth tokens
item.description: A new supply chain attack targeting the Node Package Manager (npm) ecosystem is stealing developer credentials and attempting to spread through packages published from compromised accounts. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Teams to get efficiency mode on PCs with limited resources
item.description: Microsoft is preparing to roll out a new Efficiency Mode for Microsoft Teams for systems with limited CPU and memory resources to improve app responsiveness. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Lotus Wiper Malware Targets Venezuelan Energy Systems in Destructive Attack
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented data wiper that has been used in attacks targeting Venezuela at the end of last year and the start of 2026. Dubbed Lotus Wiper, the ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Toxic Combinations: When Cross-App Permissions Stack into Risk
item.description: On January 31, 2026, researchers disclosed that Moltbook, a social network built for AI agents, had left its database wide open, exposing 35,000 email addresses and 1.5 million agent API tokens across...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft traces Universal Print issues to Graph API code change
item.description: Microsoft says that an ongoing Universal Print sharing issue that prevents users from creating some printer shares is due to a Microsoft Graph API code change. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New GoGra malware for Linux uses Microsoft Graph API for comms
item.description: A Linux variant of the GoGra backdoor uses legitimate Microsoft infrastructure, relying on an Outlook inbox for stealthy payload delivery. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Patches Critical ASP.NET Core CVE-2026-40372 Privilege Escalation Bug
item.description: Microsoft has released out-of-band updates to address a security vulnerability in ASP.NET Core that could allow an attacker to escalate privileges. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-40372, carrie...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft releases emergency patches for critical ASP.NET flaw
item.description: Microsoft has released out-of-band (OOB) security updates to patch a critical ASP.NET Core privilege escalation vulnerability. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Mustang Panda’s New LOTUSLITE Variant Targets India Banks, South Korea Policy Circles
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new variant of a known malware called LOTUSLITE that's distributed via a theme related to India's banking sector. "The backdoor communicates with a dynamic ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Cohere AI Terrarium Sandbox Flaw Enables Root Code Execution, Container Escape
item.description: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in a Python-based sandbox called Terrarium that could result in arbitrary code execution. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-5752, is rated 9.3...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Over 1,300 Microsoft SharePoint servers vulnerable to spoofing attacks
item.description: Over 1,300 Microsoft SharePoint servers exposed online remain unpatched against a spoofing vulnerability that was exploited as a zero-day and is still being abused in ongoing attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
